story.description: A clip of rivers, streams, currents, waterfalls, or freshwater systems: video of water flowing in river
story.hook: Rivers sort sediment while they bend.
story.lead: Rivers sort sediment while they bend. Watch the edge of the flow, because slower water drops heavier material while faster water keeps cutting. That sorting is how a quiet bend slowly redraws the map. Which river edge should we compare next?
story.script: Rivers sort sediment while they bend. Watch the edge of the flow, because slower water drops heavier material while faster water keeps cutting. That sorting is how a quiet bend slowly redraws the map. Which river edge should we compare next?

story.hook: Rivers reveal the rocky current first.

story.description: A clip of farm animals: charming ducks feeding on urban lawn
story.hook: Ducks reveal the waterproof feathers first.
story.lead: Ducks reveal the waterproof feathers first. Watch closely, because ducks spread oil from a gland near the tail. That coating helps water bead away, keeping the body warmer and lighter. The pond looks easy because the feather coat is engineered. Would you test it?
story.script: Ducks reveal the waterproof feathers first. Watch closely, because ducks spread oil from a gland near the tail. That coating helps water bead away, keeping the body warmer and lighter. The pond looks easy because the feather coat is engineered. Would you test it?

story.description: A clip of fungi, mushrooms, mycelium, or forest decomposition: purple texture close up with soft lighting
story.hook: Mushrooms connect through threads under the soil.
story.lead: Mushrooms connect through threads under the soil. Watch the mushroom cap, then imagine the hidden part below it. Mycelium spreads through soil as fine threads, moving nutrients while breaking down dead material. That is why the real fungal story is usually underground. Which tiny network next?

reasons: opening promise is not concrete enough
